content: Iranian naval units mined approaches to the Strait of Hormuz overnight, forcing tankers to halt transit while insurers reviewed war risk policies. Shipping companies said traffic slowed sharply after the naval incident.

The Vanguard Energy ETF rose after oil prices climbed. Analysts cited war risk insurance and disruption fears around the Strait of Hormuz, but the article focused on shares, portfolio weights, dividend yield, expense ratio, and energy company earnings.
notes: Investment commentary; should be filtered by triage.

content: Saudi officials said drones hit the Abqaiq processing plant, causing fires and temporary damage at the oil facility. Emergency crews contained the attack and exports continued through other terminals.
Saudi and UAE ground forces joined a coalition operation after troops arrived near the border. Officials described the deployment as part of a joint operation with allied response units.
Houthi attacks in the Red Sea forced merchant vessels to reroute from the Bab el-Mandeb corridor. Several cargo ships requested naval escort after missiles targeted vessels in the area.
An opinion column argued that Gulf tensions could someday threaten global trade if leaders miscalculate. It offered scenarios but reported no attack, closure, seizure, deployment, or damage.
notes: Speculative commentary; should remain low/no event.
